title: drosera intermedia f. corymbosa
intro: Drosera intermedia f. corymbosa, also known as the bog rosemary or lesser sundew, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the Droseraceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Drosera intermedia f. corymbosa include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and reduce stress. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
